visa_types.overview:
Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment
                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre. 
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application Centre in Amman, Jordan. Visa applicants
                                                                        are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Note: Third-country antionals that are legal residents in Jordan must have proof they have been living in Jordan no less than two years.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience 
Note: The passport will stay at the possession of the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan for the entire duration of the assessment. Otherwise, the applicant
                                                                        needs to contact the Embassy of the Hellenic Republic in Amman in order to proceed with the cancellation of the visa application.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment
                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre. 
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application Centre in Amman, Jordan. Visa applicants
                                                                        are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Note: Third-country antionals that are legal residents in Jordan must have proof they have been living in Jordan no less than two years.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience 
Note: The passport will stay at the possession of the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan for the entire duration of the assessment. Otherwise, the applicant
                                                                        needs to contact the Embassy of the Hellenic Republic in Amman in order to proceed with the cancellation of the visa application.

## Visa Fees

[grc > jor > en > Table:Visiting Friends / Familly > Visa Fees 1]
All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate.
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 12 years of age in the short term category.
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change once a month .
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
INFORMATION
This regime is applicable to spouses of EU-EEA citizens, minor descendants of either the EU-EEA citizen or the spouse, and major descendants or ascendants that depend economically from
                                                                        the EU-EEA relative
For a list of the required documents, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/mtJ6SlnqD7Pmx4Gu1GWip/def9290de1e70fb38cebd23429a2af30/Application-Requirements-for-Greece-ver.-26-September-2024.pdf). 
NOTE : Travel insurance should include coverage starting at least 3 days before the planned arrival to the Schengen Area and 3 days after the intended departure from the Schengen Area, due to sudden adjustments of the flight dates of the airline companies recently

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
To be decided by the embassy

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.
You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application
                                                                        form.
                                                                    
Schengen visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ukT6PlM9FqbX7ZAKH0mef/d64c6be47da6b77deb20ccb6f0d504e5/Greece-Application-2024-Final.pdf) 
Important Embassy statement (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5y6GseCbg2rSSOZ9Shw5xF/ac7469ce800ac03e3103b2056dda02c3/Consular_section.pdf) 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.name: Seamen Transit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Airport visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area. Only some nationalities require
                                                                        an Airport Transit Visa Documents to be submitted with the visa application: 
- A visa application form correctly completed and signed
- Two (02) recent color photographs (3.5 cm × 4 cm) bearing the true likeness to the applicant not older than 06 months
- International Passport (including photocopies, only pages containing any information). The passport should be valid for at least three (03) months after the expiry of the validity
                                                                            period of the visa and contain at least two (02) free pages.
- Previous passport.
- In case of a new passport, photocopies of the old passport are required
- Internal Passport (including photocopies, only pages containing any information)
- A valid entry visa for the final country of destination
- A proof of transport arrangements (confirmed airplane ticket or bookings)
Important 
- The above mentioned visa is for Airport Transit only and not valid for any other Schengen country
- Applications should as a rule, be submitted by the applicant. In special cases (distance etc) they could be submitted by any other person having a special power of attorney. The consulate
                                                                            may at any time request the presence of the applicant for an interview as well as request additional documents
NOTE : Travel insurance should cover at least 3 days before entering the Schengen Area and for at least 3 days after leaving it

                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre. 
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application Centre in Amman, Jordan. Visa applicants
                                                                        are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Note: Third-country antionals that are legal residents in Jordan must have proof they have been living in Jordan no less than two years.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience 
Note: The passport will stay at the possession of the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan for the entire duration of the assessment. Otherwise, the applicant
                                                                        needs to contact the Embassy of the Hellenic Republic in Amman in order to proceed with the cancellation of the visa application.

## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment
                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre. 
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application Centre in Amman, Jordan. Visa applicants
                                                                        are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Note: Third-country antionals that are legal residents in Jordan must have proof they have been living in Jordan no less than two years.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience 
Note: The passport will stay at the possession of the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan for the entire duration of the assessment. Otherwise, the applicant
                                                                        needs to contact the Embassy of the Hellenic Republic in Amman in order to proceed with the cancellation of the visa application.

## Visa Fees

[grc > jor > en > Table:Transit Visa > Visa Fees 1]
All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate.
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 12 years of age in the short term category.
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change once a month .
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
Airport visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area. Only some nationalities require
                                                                        an Airport Transit Visa Documents to be submitted with the visa application: 
- A visa application form correctly completed and signed
- Two (02) recent color photographs (3.5 cm × 4 cm) bearing the true likeness to the applicant not older than 06 months
- International Passport (including photocopies, only pages containing any information). The passport should be valid for at least three (03) months after the expiry of the validity
                                                                            period of the visa and contain at least two (02) free pages.
- Previous passport.
- In case of a new passport, photocopies of the old passport are required
- Internal Passport (including photocopies, only pages containing any information)
- A valid entry visa for the final country of destination
- A proof of transport arrangements (confirmed airplane ticket or bookings)
Important 
- The above mentioned visa is for Airport Transit only and not valid for any other Schengen country
- Applications should as a rule, be submitted by the applicant. In special cases (distance etc) they could be submitted by any other person having a special power of attorney. The consulate
                                                                            may at any time request the presence of the applicant for an interview as well as request additional documents
NOTE : Travel insurance should cover at least 3 days before entering the Schengen Area and for at least 3 days after leaving it

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
To be decided by the embassy

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.
You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application
                                                                        form.

Schengen visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ukT6PlM9FqbX7ZAKH0mef/d64c6be47da6b77deb20ccb6f0d504e5/Greece-Application-2024-Final.pdf) 
- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR
- Press the right button on your mouse on the required link.
- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.

visa_types.documents_required:
INFORMATION
For Persons applying for Students Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original and copy of the passport main pages (front with personal info, residence, and Schengen visas). For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos of good quality (white background).
- Visa application completed in English and signed (name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be valid for at least six (6) months from the date of visa issuance.
- Previous passport.
- Applicants should be residents of Jordan and have a valid residence of at least six (6) months (for non - Jordan and GCC citizens).
- Salary certificate and authorized signature of his which registered in Chamber of Commerce
- Original & copy of the work permit from the Ministry of Labour, Jordan (for non - Jordan and GCC citizens).
- International medical travel insurance for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30.000 Euros.
- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation / alternate accommodation
- Proof of adequate financial means: 
- Bank statement of the Parent.
- Proof of Bank account in Greece or proof of the credit card provided by the parent.
- Certificate of enrolment or Acceptance/Admission letter from the University/College stating the following details: 
- Duration of course
- Type of Course (Subjects undertaken)
NOTE : Travel insurance should include coverage starting at least 3 days before the planned arrival to the Schengen Area and 3 days after the intended departure from the Schengen Area, due to sudden adjustments of the flight dates of the airline companies recently

                                                                        for a personal Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport
                                                                        & the other relevant documents will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre. 
Only Jordan nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Jordan can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application Centre in Amman, Jordan. Visa applicants
                                                                        are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Note: Third-country antionals that are legal residents in Jordan must have proof they have been living in Jordan no less than two years.
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. 
Note: The passport will stay at the possession of the Embassy of Hellenic Republic in Amman, Jordan for the entire duration of the assessment. Otherwise, the applicant
                                                                        needs to contact the Embassy of the Hellenic Republic in Amman in order to proceed with the cancellation of the visa application.

## Visa Fees

[grc > jor > en > Table:Medical Treatment > Visa Fees 1]
All payments will be collected in local currency (cash only) as per the local exchange rate.
- Exemption of visa fee is mainly in the case of children below 12 years of age in the short term category.
- The applicable visa fee in Euro is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change once a month .
- The visa fee is non-refundable

## Documents Required
INFORMATION
For Persons applying for Medical Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original and copy of the passport main pages (front with personal info, residence, and Schengen visas). For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos of good quality (white background).
- Visa application completed in English and signed (name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be valid for at least six (6) months from the date of visa issuance.
- Previous passport.
- Applicants should be residents of Jordan and have a valid residence of at least six (6) months (for non - Jordan and GCC citizens).
- Salary certificate and authorized signature of his which registered in Chamber of Commerce
- Original & copy of the work permit from the Ministry of Labour, Jordan (for non - Jordan and GCC citizens).
- Bank statement for the last six (6) months with present balance.
- International medical travel insurance for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30.000 Euros.
- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation
- Medical report issued by the doctor in charge of the treatment in Amman, Jordan
- Written confirmation of appointment with doctor in charge of treatment in Greece
- For persons escorting the patient: confirmed hotel booking (full address of the hotel must be written in the application: full name, street name, building name & number, city and
                                                                            city code)
NOTE : Travel insurance should include coverage starting at least 3 days before the planned arrival to the Schengen Area and 3 days after the intended departure from the Schengen Area, due to sudden adjustments of the flight dates of the airline companies recently

## Photo Specifications
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
- Use plain light-colored background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure: 
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued
                                                                        to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
To be decided by the embassy

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.
You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application
                                                                        form.
                                                                    
Schengen visa application form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3ukT6PlM9FqbX7ZAKH0mef/d64c6be47da6b77deb20ccb6f0d504e5/Greece-Application-2024-Final.pdf)

- Click on the form link
- When the form opens, save the form on your computer.
OR

- Select Save Target As from the list
- Save the form on your computer.
